about_text.value: A branching surveillance dystopia about memory control, denunciation, private revolt, and the state's ability to rewrite love into evidence.

characters.name: Winston Smith
characters.description: A records clerk who keeps testing whether a private memory can survive public correction.

characters.name: Julia
characters.description: A practical dissident who hides rebellion in appetite, rhythm, and small acts of disobedience.

characters.name: O'Brien
characters.description: A cultivated Inner Party operative who can sound like a liberator while tightening the trap.

characters.name: Mr. Charrington
characters.description: A seemingly harmless antiques dealer whose rooms and objects are already under watch.

text_script.value: Every record can be revised, but private memory keeps leaving measurable residue. The room tilts toward telescreen.
